problem.passage:
3. Read the passage and follow the directions.【2 points】
In the languages of the world, we have two different types
of adposition: a preposition type (e.g., English) and a
postposition type (e.g., Korean). With the preposition type,
we find that a preposition head precedes its complement as in
(1a). As for the postposition type, a postposition follows its
complement as in (1b).
(1) a. Preposition b. Postposition
However, some linguists argue that all languages uniformly
have the preposition system and the seemingly postposition
system is derived from the preposition type by movement as
in (2).
(2)
We can apply the analysis in (2) to the so-called particle
structure.
[ this part] out t ]. (3) They left [
i i PP NP
In (3), NP this part moves from the position of
the head out to the specifier position.
Fill in the blank with the ONE most appropriate word from the
passage.
영 어 [전공A]
